Who's Who in Landau's Problems Research
Home Top 100 Location In Memoriam Genealogy Network Reading List Overlap About

Coauthorship network

Who has written papers with whom, among the Top 100

Each numbered node is a Top 100 researcher (the number is their rank), colored by region and sized by rank. A line connects two researchers who have co-authored at least one paper in the arXiv set this directory is built from; thicker lines mean more joint papers. This is the same coauthorship structure that feeds the arXiv eigenvector-centrality signal in the ranking. Drag nodes, scroll to zoom, hover for a name, click a node to select it and open its profile from the panel below (double-click opens the profile directly). Researchers with no Top 100 co-authors in the set sit apart.

● North America   ● Europe   ● Asia & Pacific   ● Other
Tip: click Freeze, then drag a node out to isolate part of the graph.

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwilp.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.

Last built June 2026.
